channel.title: How to serve static files
channel.description:
<!--
Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?
If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible
https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3
The info you may provide: - Description of your issue - Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console - A repository with the code of your project - A deployed version of your site - The URL of your WP REST API
-->
How to serve static files like `.ico` or `.js`? I tried to create a `static` folder in my theme but it didn't work when I used html tags like `<link rel="icon" href="/static/favicon.ico" />`

item.title: How to serve static files
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
baktiaditya:

i think you guys can consider adding support for .ico files in here:

Sure, that’d be great. Could you please submit a PR for that?

Read full topic

item.title: How to serve static files
item.dc:creator: baktiaditya
item.description:
hi, thanks for the reply.

i solved it using custom webpack configuration based on this thread Customize Webpack configuration

and i think you guys can consider adding support for .ico files in here:

github.com

frontity/frontity/blob/c5b4f80e0764c01b91824c251e38dda0781974c8/packages/core/src/config/webpack/modules.ts#L79-L97

    
      
    
          
  1.   test: /\.(png|jpe?g|gif|svg)$/,
    2. 
          
  2.   use: [
    3. 
          
  3.     {
    4. 
          
  4.       loader: "file-loader",
    5. 
          
  5.       options: {
    6. 
          
  6.         name: (file: string): string => {
    7. 
          
  7.           const filename = /([^/\\]+)\.(?:png|jpe?g|gif|svg)$/.exec(
    8. 
          
  8.             file
    9. 
          
  9.           ) || ["", "image"];
    10. 
          
  10.           return mode === "development"
    11. 
          
  11.             ? `${filename[1]}.[ext]`
    12. 
          
  12.             : `${filename[1]}-[hash].[ext]`;
    13. 
          
  13.         },
    14. 
          
  14.         outputPath: "images",
    15. 
          
  15.         emitFile: target !== "server",
    16. 
          
  16.       },
    17. 
          
  17.     },
    18. 
          
  18.   ],
    19. 
          
  19. },
    20.

Read full topic

item.title: How to serve static files
item.dc:creator: mburridge
item.description:
Hi @baktiaditya

Welcome to the Frontity community.

See this thread, and in particular this answer, this answer and this answer which may guide you toward a solution. In short, you should import the static file into a React component and use it there.

Read full topic

item.title: How to serve static files
item.dc:creator: baktiaditya
item.description:
How to serve static files like .ico or .js?
I tried to create a static folder in my theme but it didn’t work when I used html tags like <link rel="icon" href="/static/favicon.ico" />

Read full topic
